step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the previous conclusion. Additionally, while torsades de pointes (TdP) can be caused by electrolyte imbalances among other factors, the combination of two potent negative chronotropics—metoprolol and verapamil—is less likely to directly induce long QT intervals necessary for TdP development compared to bradyarrhythmias seen here. Thus, focusing on bradycardia and AV blocks aligns better given these specific drug interactions.
```plaintext
